div.head: HO–9. Editorial Note
div.p: On July 12, 1959, Armando Velásquez Cerrato, the former Chief of Staff of the Honduran Armed Forces, attempted once again to overthrow the government of President Villeda Morales, launching a full scale revolt in Tegucigalpa with the assistance of disloyal police and military units. The bulk of the Honduran Armed Forces remained loyal to President Villeda and acted successfully to crush the revolt and to force Col. Velásquez to seek asylum in the Costa Rican Embassy. Documentation on this incident is in Department of State file 715.00.
